questions.question: Why did Rakesh’s parents send him to live with his grandfather?
questions.answer: Because there was no school in their village and they wanted Rakesh to get an education.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh decide to plant the cherry seed?
questions.answer: He wanted to see if the seed would grow into a cherry tree.

questions.question: How did Rakesh discover that the cherry seed had grown?
questions.answer: He noticed a small green shoot growing from the soil.

questions.question: Why did Rakesh place pebbles around the young plant?
questions.answer: He placed pebbles around it to protect it from being stepped on.

questions.question: How did the goat affect the cherry tree?
questions.answer: The goat ate all the leaves of the young cherry tree.

questions.question: What happened when the grass cutter accidentally cut the tree?
questions.answer: The tree was cut in two but later grew again.

questions.question: How did the cherry tree show resilience?
questions.answer: Despite being damaged, it continued to grow new shoots and leaves.

questions.question: Why was Rakesh interested in watching the cherry tree grow?
questions.answer: Because he had planted it himself and wanted to see how it developed.

questions.question: How did the monsoon season affect the cherry tree?
questions.answer: The monsoon rains helped the tree grow faster and stronger.

questions.question: What surprised Rakesh about the tree’s growth?
questions.answer: He was surprised that the tree survived many difficulties and continued to grow.

questions.question: Why did insects and birds visit the cherry tree?
questions.answer: They visited the tree because it provided food and shelter.

questions.question: Why was the appearance of the blossom important?
questions.answer: It showed that the tree had grown successfully after many years.

questions.question: How did Rakesh feel when he saw the blossoms?
questions.answer: He felt happy and proud.

questions.question: Why were the cherries not enjoyable for Rakesh?
questions.answer: Because they tasted sour.

questions.question: Why did birds enjoy the cherries?
questions.answer: Because the cherries were natural food for them.

questions.question: What does the cherry tree represent for Rakesh?
questions.answer: It represents his effort and achievement.

questions.question: How did Rakesh and his grandfather spend time together?
questions.answer: They enjoyed watching the cherry tree grow.

questions.question: Why did grandfather enjoy sitting under the cherry tree?
questions.answer: Because it gave shade and was beautiful.

questions.question: What lesson did Rakesh learn from the cherry tree?
questions.answer: He learned that patience and care help things grow.

questions.question: How does the story show the passage of time?
questions.answer: Through the changing seasons and the gradual growth of the tree.

questions.question: What role did nature play in the story?
questions.answer: Nature helped the tree grow and showed its beauty and strength.

questions.question: Why is the cherry tree special to Rakesh?
questions.answer: Because he planted and cared for it himself.

questions.question: What does the story suggest about caring for plants?
questions.answer: It shows that caring for plants requires patience and responsibility.

questions.question: How does the cherry tree show the power of nature?
questions.answer: It continues to grow even after being damaged.

questions.question: What is the main message of 'The Cherry Tree'?
questions.answer: The story teaches patience, perseverance, and love for nature.
